steps.step: 1. Fry the mince in a pan with a little oil.
2. Add the chopped tomatoes & tomato puree. Mix to combine.
3. Cook the mince & tomatoes for roughly 10 - 12 minutes.
4. Place your spaghetti on to cook. Cook until desired toughness.
5. Plate up and serve.

ingredients.ingredient: 8g or one packet Dried Instant Yeast

steps.step: 1. Mix the water, oil and yeast together. Leave for 5 minutes.
2. In the meantime place your flour and salt into a bowl.
3. Once ready, pour the yeast mix into the flour.
4. Mix with your hands until combined.
5. Tip onto floured work surface and kneed for 15 minutes.
6. Place into lightly oiled bowl. Cover with cling film and leave to rise for an hour.
7. Once risen, place into parchment lined loaf tin, cover and leave to rise again for 30-40 minutes.
8. Uncover and bake in the oven at 200C for 20-30 minutes or until golden brown.
9. Wait until cool and slice to desired thickness as required.

name: Chicken & Leek Pie
desc: Chicken, leek and delicious gravy encased in pastry.

ingredients.ingredient: Shortcrust Pastry (Or puff pastry for the lid if available)
Enough gravy to fill your pie dish

steps.step: 1. Roll out 2/3 of your pastry so it covers the whole of your pie dish.
2. Combine your leeks and stock in a pan. Bring to the boil and simmer until leeks are soft.
3. In the interim, fry your chicken in a pan with a little oil.
4. Once both are nearly complete, make up your gravy.
5. Place pastry in the pie dish if not done already. Put in your chicken and leeks, then cover with your gravy.
6. Roll out the rest of your pastry to form a lid for the pie dish.
7. Cover your pie with the lid. Poke several small holes in the lid and bake in the oven at 200C for about 20-25 minutes.
8. Wait to cool slightly before diving and serving.
1. Cream your sugar and butter together.
2. Mix in the eggs, one at a time, adding about a tablespoon of the flour each time.
3. Add the remaining flour and mix until combined.
4. Grease and line two sandwich tins.
5. Pour half the mixture into each tin. If your tins are too small bake what you have then reuse the tins later.
6. Bake in the oven at 200C for about 10-12 minutes or until a skewer comes out clean when inserted.
7. Allow to cool. Then layer each cake layer with your choice of filling in between.
8. Slice and serve.

name: Cajun Chicken
desc: One pot goodness with a cajun kick.

steps.step: 1. Fry the chicken and onion in a pan with a little oil.
2. Add the pepper & rice, and fry for another minute or two.
3. Add the seasoning, chopped tomatoes and water. Bring to the boil then cover & simmer for around 20 minutes or until water is absorbed and rice is cooked
4. Serve and enjoy.
